question: How do I find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t spot my name there?
answer_agent:
If you can’t spot your name on the leaderboard, click the **“Jump to your record on the leaderboard”** link — you were automatically assigned a random display name when you set up your account.

If you want to see or change the name that appears publicly, go to **“Edit Course Profile”**:
- **First field:** your leaderboard/display name
- **Second field:** your official name for the certificate
answer_orig:
When you set up your account, you are automatically assigned a random name, such as “Lucid Elbakyan.” Click on the "Jump to your record on the leaderboard" link to find your entry.

If you want to see what your Display name is, click on the "Edit Course Profile" button.

<{IMAGE:image_1}>

- **First field:** This is your nickname/displayed name. You can change it if you want to be known by your Slack username, GitHub username, or any other nickname of your choice. This is useful if you want to remain anonymous.
- **Second field:** Change this to your official name as in your identification documents—passport, national ID card, driver's license, etc. This is mandatory if you do not want "Lucid Elbakyan" on your certificate. This name will appear on your Certificate!
tool_calls: [{'name': 'search', 'arguments': '{"query":"find my own entry on the leaderboard if I can’t spot my name there"}'}]

question: Why do I have a random display name on the course leaderboard, and can I change it?
answer_agent:
Yes — that random name is your **default public display name** on the leaderboard. It’s assigned automatically when you create your account.

You **can change it**:

- Go to **Edit Course Profile**
- The **first field** is your **nickname / displayed name** for the leaderboard
- You can replace the random name with something like your **Slack username**, **GitHub username**, or any nickname you prefer

If you also want your **certificate name** to be correct, update the **second field** with your official name exactly as it appears on your ID/passport, since that’s the name used on the certificate.

question: Can I take this course on my own pace and still get the certificate, or is it only for the live group?
answer_agent:
No — you can follow the course in self-paced mode, but you won’t get a certificate that way.

According to the FAQ, certificates are only awarded if you finish the course with a live cohort, because the capstone requires peer review, and peer review is only available while the course is actively running.
answer_orig:
No, you can only get a certificate if you finish the course with a "live" cohort.

We don't award certificates for the self-paced mode. The reason is you need to peer-review 3 capstone(s) after submitting your project.

You can only peer-review projects at the time the course is running; after the form is closed and the peer-review list is compiled.

question: Why can’t self-paced students get a certificate at the end?
answer_agent:
Self-paced students can’t get a certificate because the course certificate depends on participating in the live cohort’s peer-review process.

According to the FAQ, you need to:
- submit your project during the course run, and
- peer-review 3 other capstone projects

That only works while the course is actively running, since the peer-review forms close afterward and the review list is compiled. So certificates aren’t awarded in self-paced mode.

question: Why does the homework content keep changing before the deadline?
answer_agent:
The homework may still be changing if it’s not finalized yet.

Specifically:
- If the homework title contains **`[DRAFT]`**, it’s **not ready**.
- It’s considered ready only when **both** are true:
  - the homework form is open on the course platform
  - the title **does not** contain **`[DRAFT]`**

Until then, the content can change, so working on it early is at your own risk because the final version may be different.
